{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Internal Control Manager",
  "description": "Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc has been a part of the lives of Nigerians at home and abroad. Our global vision is to be a leading foods company in Africa providing high quality and affordable products in the most convenient ways to consumers through world class brands like Golden Penny Flour, Golden Penny Semovita, Goldenvita, Golden Pasta and Golden Noodles. The Company&#39;s flagship brand, Golden Penny, remains one of the best known and the preferred brands amongst bakers, confectioneries and consumers in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Internal Control Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Nigeria

Purpose


	To monitor inventory control processes laid down by management and appraise independently all controls within stores, procurement, production, loading and packing areas of the organization.


The Job?


	Planning, organizing and supervising the inventory control operations and providing quality assurance on work done by the team.
	Provide leadership and direction in communicating and monitoring inventory control policies and processes.
	Ensure all materials received by the company are following policies and procedures.
	Weekly reconciliation of material movements including report on any shortages.
	Perform administrative tasks such as allocation of staff to various duty posts, approval of staff overtime, dissemination of relevant information to team members.
	Check compliance with approved operational procedures at entry gate, weighbridge, loading bays and exit gates.
	Report deficiencies in control to the manager and make appropriate recommendations.
	Preparation and circulation of monthly inventory count schedule.


Qualifications


	HND/Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Professional Qualification Required: ACCA, ACA or a recognized accounting certification.


Experience:?


	Minimum of ten years&rsquo; experience in internal control, audit, inventory management, or related operational roles.
	Experience in a structured, fast-paced manufacturing or FMCG environment is highly required.


The Person Must?:


	Possess strong knowledge of internal control frameworks and compliance procedures.
	Demonstrate excellent understanding of inventory management and materials movement processes.
	Be highly proficient in financial analysis, reconciliation, and variance investigation.
	Have strong audit capabilities with the ability to conduct independent control reviews.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-04",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-05",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "name": "Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc"
    
  },

Internal Control Manager Job at Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc




        
    

        
        Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc has been a part of the lives of Nigerians at home and abroad. Our global vision is to be a leading foods company in Africa providing high quality and affordable products in the most convenient ways to consumers through world class brands like Golden Penny Flour, Golden Penny Semovita, Goldenvita, Golden Pasta and Golden Noodles. The Company's flagship brand, Golden Penny, remains one of the best known and the preferred brands amongst bakers, confectioneries and consumers in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Control Manager

Job Location: Nigeria

Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	To monitor inventory control processes laid down by management and appraise independently all controls within stores, procurement, production, loading and packing areas of the organization.


The Job?


	Planning, organizing and supervising the inventory control operations and providing quality assurance on work done by the team.
	Provide leadership and direction in communicating and monitoring inventory control policies and processes.
	Ensure all materials received by the company are following policies and procedures.
	Weekly reconciliation of material movements including report on any shortages.
	Perform administrative tasks such as allocation of staff to various duty posts, approval of staff overtime, dissemination of relevant information to team members.
	Check compliance with approved operational procedures at entry gate, weighbridge, loading bays and exit gates.
	Report deficiencies in control to the manager and make appropriate recommendations.
	Preparation and circulation of monthly inventory count schedule.


Qualifications


	HND/Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Business Administration, or a related field.
	Professional Qualification Required: ACCA, ACA or a recognized accounting certification.


Experience:?


	Minimum of ten years’ experience in internal control, audit, inventory management, or related operational roles.
	Experience in a structured, fast-paced manufacturing or FMCG environment is highly required.


The Person Must?:


	Possess strong knowledge of internal control frameworks and compliance procedures.
	Demonstrate excellent understanding of inventory management and materials movement processes.
	Be highly proficient in financial analysis, reconciliation, and variance investigation.
	Have strong audit capabilities with the ability to conduct independent control reviews.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Logistics Supervisor",
  "description": "Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc has been a part of the lives of Nigerians at home and abroad. Our global vision is to be a leading foods company in Africa providing high quality and affordable products in the most convenient ways to consumers through world class brands like Golden Penny Flour, Golden Penny Semovita, Goldenvita, Golden Pasta and Golden Noodles. The Company&#39;s flagship brand, Golden Penny, remains one of the best known and the preferred brands amongst bakers, confectioneries and consumers in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Logistics Supervisor

Job Location:&nbsp;Calabar, Cross Rivers

Purpose


	To&nbsp;Supervise and coordinate logistics operations, ensuring the efficient transportation, warehousing, and distribution of goods while optimizing costs and maintaining compliance with regulatory and company policies.


The Job?


	Supervise and coordinate daily logistics operations, including transportation, warehousing, and distribution.
	Ensure timely and cost-effective delivery of goods to customers and internal stakeholders.
	Monitor and track shipments, resolving any delays or issues promptly.
	Oversee the scheduling and dispatching of trucks for optimal efficiency.
	Maintain accurate records of shipments, inventory, and logistics expenses.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, safety regulations, and industry standards.
	Collaborate with transporters, suppliers, and internal teams to enhance logistics processes.
	Conduct periodic audits to ensure adherence to logistics and inventory management policies.


The person must


	exposure in logistics operations, including transportation, warehousing, or distribution.
	Basic knowledge of shipment tracking, inventory management, and record-keeping.
	Ability to monitor daily operations and help resolve minor delays or issues.
	Good organizational and time management skills to handle multiple tasks efficiently.
	Willingness to learn, follow processes, and adhere to company policies and safety standards.
	Strong communication and teamwork skills to coordinate with colleagues, suppliers, and transporters.


Qualifications?


	Strictly Diploma /&nbsp;OND holders in Supply Chain or in a related field.


Experience:?


	2 years of experience in logistics, supply chain, or transportation management preferably in a food manufacturing or FMCG environment.

Logistics Supervisor Job at Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc




        
    

        
        Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc has been a part of the lives of Nigerians at home and abroad. Our global vision is to be a leading foods company in Africa providing high quality and affordable products in the most convenient ways to consumers through world class brands like Golden Penny Flour, Golden Penny Semovita, Goldenvita, Golden Pasta and Golden Noodles. The Company's flagship brand, Golden Penny, remains one of the best known and the preferred brands amongst bakers, confectioneries and consumers in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Logistics Supervisor

Job Location: Calabar, Cross Rivers

Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	To Supervise and coordinate logistics operations, ensuring the efficient transportation, warehousing, and distribution of goods while optimizing costs and maintaining compliance with regulatory and company policies.


The Job?


	Supervise and coordinate daily logistics operations, including transportation, warehousing, and distribution.
	Ensure timely and cost-effective delivery of goods to customers and internal stakeholders.
	Monitor and track shipments, resolving any delays or issues promptly.
	Oversee the scheduling and dispatching of trucks for optimal efficiency.
	Maintain accurate records of shipments, inventory, and logistics expenses.
	Ensure compliance with company policies, safety regulations, and industry standards.
	Collaborate with transporters, suppliers, and internal teams to enhance logistics processes.
	Conduct periodic audits to ensure adherence to logistics and inventory management policies.


The person must


	exposure in logistics operations, including transportation, warehousing, or distribution.
	Basic knowledge of shipment tracking, inventory management, and record-keeping.
	Ability to monitor daily operations and help resolve minor delays or issues.
	Good organizational and time management skills to handle multiple tasks efficiently.
	Willingness to learn, follow processes, and adhere to company policies and safety standards.
	Strong communication and teamwork skills to coordinate with colleagues, suppliers, and transporters.


Qualifications?


	Strictly Diploma / OND holders in Supply Chain or in a related field.


Experience:?


	2 years of experience in logistics, supply chain, or transportation management preferably in a food manufacturing or FMCG environment.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

The Company&#39;s flagship brand, Golden Penny, remains one of the best known and the preferred brands amongst bakers, confectioneries and consumers in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;HR Advisor

Job Location:&nbsp;Nigeria

Purpose


	To provide first-line HR advisory support across assigned business units, ensuring effective implementation of HR strategies, policies, and initiatives while supporting the delivery of Group HR projects and partnering with business leaders to drive people performance.


The Job?


	Provide first-line HR advisory support to employees across assigned plants, warehouses, and business functions on employee relations, HR processes, and specialist HR services.
	Monitor workforce levels to ensure optimal manning and headcount efficiency.
	Track, maintain, and report agreed HR productivity metrics.
	Collaborate with Centre of Excellence and other HR teams to improve employee benefits and service delivery.
	Coordinate the performance appraisal process, ensuring timely completion across assigned units.
	Support managers in implementing Performance Improvement Plans (PIPs) where required.
	Monitor Learning &amp; Development interventions to close identified competency gaps.
	Drive effective communication and employee buy-in for change initiatives.
	Monitor attendance, absenteeism, and overtime, ensuring compliance with company policy.
	Prepare and integrate HR reports into weekly business reports.
	Develop HR dashboards and reports for the HR Business Partner.
	Support any other HR initiatives as assigned.


Qualifications?


	First Degree in Human Resources, Industrial Relations, Business Administration, or a related discipline.


Experience:


	Minimum of 2 years of relevant HR experience.
	Experience in manufacturing, FMCG, or industrial operations is an added advantage.


The person must:


	Possess sound knowledge of HR policies, employee relations, and HR best practices.
	Demonstrate strong interpersonal and communication skills.
	Have excellent problem-solving and conflict-resolution abilities.
	Be highly organized with strong reporting and analytical skills.
	Be proactive, adaptable, and able to manage multiple priorities.
	Have the ability to work effectively in a fast-paced operational environment.

HR Advisor Job at Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc




        
    

        
        Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc has been a part of the lives of Nigerians at home and abroad. Our global vision is to be a leading foods company in Africa providing high quality and affordable products in the most convenient ways to consumers through world class brands like Golden Penny Flour, Golden Penny Semovita, Goldenvita, Golden Pasta and Golden Noodles. The Company's flagship brand, Golden Penny, remains one of the best known and the preferred brands amongst bakers, confectioneries and consumers in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Advisor

Job Location: Nigeria

Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	To provide first-line HR advisory support across assigned business units, ensuring effective implementation of HR strategies, policies, and initiatives while supporting the delivery of Group HR projects and partnering with business leaders to drive people performance.


The Job?


	Provide first-line HR advisory support to employees across assigned plants, warehouses, and business functions on employee relations, HR processes, and specialist HR services.
	Monitor workforce levels to ensure optimal manning and headcount efficiency.
	Track, maintain, and report agreed HR productivity metrics.
	Collaborate with Centre of Excellence and other HR teams to improve employee benefits and service delivery.
	Coordinate the performance appraisal process, ensuring timely completion across assigned units.
	Support managers in implementing Performance Improvement Plans (PIPs) where required.
	Monitor Learning & Development interventions to close identified competency gaps.
	Drive effective communication and employee buy-in for change initiatives.
	Monitor attendance, absenteeism, and overtime, ensuring compliance with company policy.
	Prepare and integrate HR reports into weekly business reports.
	Develop HR dashboards and reports for the HR Business Partner.
	Support any other HR initiatives as assigned.


Qualifications?


	First Degree in Human Resources, Industrial Relations, Business Administration, or a related discipline.


Experience:


	Minimum of 2 years of relevant HR experience.
	Experience in manufacturing, FMCG, or industrial operations is an added advantage.


The person must:


	Possess sound knowledge of HR policies, employee relations, and HR best practices.
	Demonstrate strong interpersonal and communication skills.
	Have excellent problem-solving and conflict-resolution abilities.
	Be highly organized with strong reporting and analytical skills.
	Be proactive, adaptable, and able to manage multiple priorities.
	Have the ability to work effectively in a fast-paced operational environment.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Project Manager",
  "description": "Five28 is a top tier property development company whose aim is to be globally recognized for our innovative and timeless approach to creating aesthetically pleasing and functional spaces that elevate the way people live, work or play, telling each client&rsquo;s unique story and exceeding their expectations.

We are a privately held and incorporated organization in Nigeria with focal engagement in new builds, renovations and interior design. We provide bespoke property development and design solutions which cut across a range of residential and commercial properties. Our competitive edge over the years includes our core strengths, unrivalled local expertise, and extensive local and international network.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Project Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Plan and execute projects according to approved designs, timelines, and budgets.
	Manage all on-site activities, ensuring adherence to schedule, quality standards, and safety protocols.
	Collaborate closely with the design team to translate concepts into actionable plans.
	Coordinate with contractors, subcontractors, and suppliers to ensure timely delivery of materials and services.
	Monitor budgets and resources, taking corrective actions to prevent overruns.
	Prepare project reports and maintain accurate documentation.
	Lead, supervise, and motivate on-site teams to achieve project objectives.
	Perform any other duties aligned with the role as assigned by the Company.


Qualifications &amp; Skills


	Proven experience in project management, preferably in interior design, construction, or related fields.
	Degree in Architecture
	Strong leadership and team management skills.
	Excellent organizational, planning, and problem-solving abilities.
	Effective communication and collaboration skills.
	Familiarity with project management software and tools is a plus.


Salary
N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;chinedum@five28.com.ng&nbsp;using &quot;Project Manager Application&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Project Manager Job at Five28




        
    

        
        Five28 is a top tier property development company whose aim is to be globally recognized for our innovative and timeless approach to creating aesthetically pleasing and functional spaces that elevate the way people live, work or play, telling each client’s unique story and exceeding their expectations.

We are a privately held and incorporated organization in Nigeria with focal engagement in new builds, renovations and interior design. We provide bespoke property development and design solutions which cut across a range of residential and commercial properties. Our competitive edge over the years includes our core strengths, unrivalled local expertise, and extensive local and international network.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Position: Project Manager

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Plan and execute projects according to approved designs, timelines, and budgets.
	Manage all on-site activities, ensuring adherence to schedule, quality standards, and safety protocols.
	Collaborate closely with the design team to translate concepts into actionable plans.
	Coordinate with contractors, subcontractors, and suppliers to ensure timely delivery of materials and services.
	Monitor budgets and resources, taking corrective actions to prevent overruns.
	Prepare project reports and maintain accurate documentation.
	Lead, supervise, and motivate on-site teams to achieve project objectives.
	Perform any other duties aligned with the role as assigned by the Company.


Qualifications & Skills


	Proven experience in project management, preferably in interior design, construction, or related fields.
	Degree in Architecture
	Strong leadership and team management skills.
	Excellent organizational, planning, and problem-solving abilities.
	Effective communication and collaboration skills.
	Familiarity with project management software and tools is a plus.


Salary
N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Project Manager Application" as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Operations Supervisor",
  "description": "Total Data Limited is a management consultancy firm specializing in the provision of Human Resources and Manpower solutions to its&rsquo; clients across Nigeria. We focus on providing additional and exceptional services through value added, and end-to-end HR services to our clients; helping them maximize and build operational efficiency.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Supervisor

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Description


	We are looking to hire a competent and hands-on Operations Supervisor to oversee both warehouse and production activities within the plant.
	The role requires a detail-oriented professional who can coordinate daily operations, drive efficiency, and ensure smooth collaboration across production, warehouse, and logistics functions.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for maintaining operational standards, ensuring safety compliance, and supporting continuous improvement initiatives within a fast-paced manufacturing environment.


Job Objectives


	Oversee and coordinate daily plant operations, including production and warehouse activities, to achieve set targets and operational efficiency.
	Monitor workforce productivity, operational processes, and logistics coordination to ensure seamless workflow across departments.
	Ensure strict adherence to safety regulations, company policies, and operational procedures.
	Maintain accurate operational data and reports to support performance tracking and management decision-making.


Responsibilities


	Supervise daily production and warehouse operations to ensure schedules and targets are consistently met.
	Coordinate activities across production, warehouse, and logistics teams to ensure efficient material flow and operations.
	Monitor production processes, machinery, and workflows to minimize downtime and improve overall efficiency.
	Supervise operational staff, including production workers, warehouse personnel, loaders, and technicians.

Operations Supervisor Job at Total Data Limited




        
    

        
        Total Data Limited is a management consultancy firm specializing in the provision of Human Resources and Manpower solutions to its’ clients across Nigeria. We focus on providing additional and exceptional services through value added, and end-to-end HR services to our clients; helping them maximize and build operational efficiency.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Operations Supervisor

Job Location: Lagos

Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are looking to hire a competent and hands-on Operations Supervisor to oversee both warehouse and production activities within the plant.
	The role requires a detail-oriented professional who can coordinate daily operations, drive efficiency, and ensure smooth collaboration across production, warehouse, and logistics functions.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for maintaining operational standards, ensuring safety compliance, and supporting continuous improvement initiatives within a fast-paced manufacturing environment.


Job Objectives


	Oversee and coordinate daily plant operations, including production and warehouse activities, to achieve set targets and operational efficiency.
	Monitor workforce productivity, operational processes, and logistics coordination to ensure seamless workflow across departments.
	Ensure strict adherence to safety regulations, company policies, and operational procedures.
	Maintain accurate operational data and reports to support performance tracking and management decision-making.


Responsibilities


	Supervise daily production and warehouse operations to ensure schedules and targets are consistently met.
	Coordinate activities across production, warehouse, and logistics teams to ensure efficient material flow and operations.
	Monitor production processes, machinery, and workflows to minimize downtime and improve overall efficiency.
	Supervise operational staff, including production workers, warehouse personnel, loaders, and technicians.
	Assign tasks, manage shifts, and monitor team performance to ensure productivity and discipline.
	Provide guidance, on-the-job training, and support to staff on operational procedures and safety standards.
	Ensure full compliance with company policies, standard operating procedures, and workplace regulations.
	Enforce safety practices, including the proper use of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE).
	Maintain a clean, safe, and well-organized working environment across the plant and warehouse.
	Monitor product quality to ensure it meets internal standards and regulatory requirements.
	Ensure all machinery and equipment are functioning effectively; promptly report faults to the maintenance team.
	Oversee the proper use and handling of tools and equipment to prevent damage, accidents, or disruptions.
	Maintain accurate records of production output, inventory movements, workforce attendance, and incidents.
	Prepare and submit daily, weekly, and monthly operational reports to management.
	Identify opportunities to improve processes, enhance productivity, and reduce operational inefficiencies.
	Implement corrective measures where performance targets or quality standards are not achieved.
	Support management in executing operational strategies and continuous improvement initiatives.
	Ensure the confidentiality and security of company and operational information at all times.
	Carry out any other duties as assigned by management.


Requirements


	Minimum of First Degree (or equivalent qualification) is required.
	2 - 3 years of relevant experience in operations, production, plant supervision, or a similar industrial environment.


Key Competency Requirements
Knowledge:


	Strong understanding of production and plant operations
	Knowledge of industrial relations and workforce supervision
	Awareness of safety standards and regulatory compliance requirements
	Understanding of operational efficiency and business performance management
	Ability to manage multiple operational priorities effectively


Skills & Competencies:


	Strong supervisory and leadership capabilities
	Excellent time management and organizational skills
	Strong interpersonal and team management abilities
	Effective written and verbal communication skills
	Strong problem-solving and analytical thinking skills
	High level of attention to detail and accuracy
	Resourceful, resilient, and adaptable in a fast-paced environment
	Strong coordination and operational management skills.


Benefits


	Remuneration: N150,000.00 - N175,000.00 monthly Gross (depending on experience and competence).





Other Benefits:


	Pension remittance in line with statutory requirements
	13th-month salary
	Annual paid leave (as applicable under company policy)
	Opportunities for career growth and development within the organization
	Group Life Insurance.
